question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 raises the IS-IS metric on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (R3↔R4 primary)
from 10 to 16777214 (max). R4's matching metric stays at 10. The
primary path metric becomes 16777224 (10 + 16777214), far higher than
the backup R2→R6→R7→R4 path metric (60). SPF promotes the backup as
the steady-state best path; the primary stays in the LSDB but is
effectively shunned. Subtle traffic-engineering misconfig — no
adjacency drop, no syslog.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.103 (R3), the IS-IS metric on GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1 (the R3<->R4 primary) was raised to 16777214 (max) while R4's side stays at 10.
IS-IS path cost is the sum of egress metrics, so the asymmetric metric drives the primary path cost (~16777224) far above the backup R2->R6->R7->R4 path cost (60). SPF therefore selects the cheaper backup as the steady-state best path and shuns the primary, which remains in the LSDB but carries no traffic - a subtle traffic-engineering misconfig.
Fix: restore the IS-IS metric to 10 on R3's GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1.
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario; adjacency
stays Up and only the link metric mutates. Diagnostic ground truth
lives in expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R3 `show running-config router isis LAB interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1`: metric 16777214 (baseline: 10). R4's matching interface still advertises metric 10 — asymmetric metric on a point-to-point link.
R2 `show isis database verbose` for R3's LSPID: R3 advertises metric 16777214 for the R3-R4 link (Extended IS Reachability TLV).
Baseline pattern is symmetric (every primary metric=10, every backup metric=20). R3's 16777214 is an obvious outlier when diffed against all other interface-level metrics.
R2 `show route 192.0.2.4` (R4 loopback): now via 192.0.0.13 (R6, backup) with composite metric ~60 (baseline: primary R2-R3-R4 path).
End-to-end ping R2→R4 still succeeds — the failure is silent traffic-engineering misroute, not a black hole.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  metric 16777214

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface GigabitEthernet0/0/0/1
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  metric 10
